refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency is within 150 ms p95 to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies a high-level latency requirement for teleoperation. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) clarifies the performance target and safety relevance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end communication latency under agreed network conditions during teleoperation scenarios and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication transmission delay between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure reliable data delivery with minimal retransmissions.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and control tasking with low processing latency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols with low overhead and prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge processing modules to reduce data processing latency near the vehicle.
DP3: Employ dedicated communication channels with QoS guarantees.
DP4: Use data compression and efficient encoding to reduce payload size.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols with low overhead and prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between communication protocol and vehicle telemetry data format.
Dependency on network link quality and safety services for alerts.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication optimization, minimizing cross-domain coupling while enabling modular improvements.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline for latency measurement.
Risk that protocol optimization alone may not meet latency under degraded network conditions.
Safety criticality requires fallback mechanisms if latency thresholds are exceeded.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge processing (DP2) if latency targets are not met by protocol optimization alone.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication protocols, data prioritization, and latency optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange between vehicle and teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Control commands from teleoperation module to vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting teleoperation data transmission.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope; contains teleoperation but with low containment specificity.
Vehicle handles telemetry but not protocol optimization; low containment for DP.
Primary owner of teleoperation communication logic; best containment and minimal diffusion.
Safety alerts interface but no direct role in latency optimization.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes communication protocol logic, minimizing diffusion and enabling focused ownership.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact from dependencies on Vehicle telemetry processing.
Risk of interface mismatches if Teleoperation module changes protocol without Vehicle updates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts and synchronization mechanisms between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by targeting communication latency reduction through protocol optimization.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Dependency on Vehicle telemetry interface requires coordination.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and clear ownership for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across Teleoperation and Vehicle modules using known interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear mapping from FR to DP to IM with explicit interface definitions.

recommended_revisions: Define interface contracts between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to mitigate integration risks.
Plan fallback strategies in Safety Services for latency threshold breaches.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the safety-critical latency requirement with clear ownership and verifiable interfaces. No further decomposition needed at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication transmission delay between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure reliable data delivery with minimal retransmissions.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and control tasking with low processing latency.

revise.why: Current FR, DP, and IM assignments are consistent and meet criteria.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed baseline.
Teleoperation module can implement protocol optimizations effectively.
open_risks: Latency may exceed threshold under degraded network conditions.
Interface synchronization issues between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance test results under representative network conditions.
Interface compliance verification between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
